name: aws-cost-cleanup
description: Automated cleanup of unused AWS resources to reduce costs
capabilities: Identify unattached EBS volumes and calculate potential savings
Detect and clean up old EBS snapshots older than 90 days
Find and release unused Elastic IP addresses
Create S3 lifecycle policies for cost optimization
Generate cost impact reports for cleanup operations
Set up automated Lambda functions for scheduled cleanup
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All static findings are false positives. The skill uses legitimate AWS CLI commands for cost optimization. Network calls are to official AWS documentation URLs only. AWS service names flagged as C2 keywords are standard terminology. This is a valid DevOps tool for AWS cost cleanup.
valueStatement: Automate identification and removal of unused AWS resources to eliminate waste and reduce monthly cloud bills.
limitations: Does not delete resources that are actively in use
Requires AWS credentials with appropriate IAM permissions
Cannot recover accidentally deleted resources without backups
May require manual review for resources with dependencies

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Cost Optimization
useCases.description: Automate weekly cleanup of unused resources across multiple AWS accounts to reduce monthly cloud spending.

useCases.targetUser: Finance and operations teams tracking cloud costs
useCases.title: Cloud FinOps Teams
useCases.description: Generate reports on unused resources and potential savings to inform budget decisions and cost allocation.

useCases.targetUser: Small teams and startups with limited cloud expertise
useCases.title: Startup Cloud Management
useCases.description: Quickly identify and remove test and dev resources that are no longer needed to keep cloud bills manageable.

promptTemplates.title: Find unused AWS resources
promptTemplates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to find all unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, and old snapshots in my AWS account. Generate a report showing potential monthly savings.

promptTemplates.title: Create cleanup script
promptTemplates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to create a bash script that identifies unattached EBS volumes older than 30 days and calculates the total size and cost impact.
promptTemplates.scenario: Generate a cleanup script for review

promptTemplates.title: Set up automated cleanup
promptTemplates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to create a Lambda function that runs weekly to identify and delete unattached EBS volumes older than 7 days. Include dry-run mode.

promptTemplates.title: Cost impact analysis
promptTemplates.prompt: Use aws-cost-cleanup to calculate the monthly and annual cost savings if we clean up all unattached EBS volumes, unused Elastic IPs, and snapshots older than 90 days.

bestPractices: Always run cleanup scripts in dry-run mode first to review what will be deleted
Use resource tags to identify owners before deletion and notify stakeholders
Create final snapshots of critical data before deletion as a safety net
antiPatterns: Deleting resources without reviewing the cleanup list first
Ignoring resource dependencies - check for attached ENIs before deleting volumes
Skipping the safety checklist - always verify resources are truly unused

faq.question: Will this accidentally delete resources I still need?
faq.answer: The skill includes dry-run mode by default. All deletion commands are commented out until you explicitly enable them. Always review the output before removing comments.

faq.question: What AWS permissions are required?
faq.answer: You need read access (describe-*) for discovery and write access (delete-*) for cleanup. Use IAM roles with least privilege.

faq.question: Can this run across multiple AWS accounts?
faq.answer: Yes, the skill supports AWS Organizations integration. You can iterate across all accounts in your organization.

faq.question: How do I recover accidentally deleted resources?
faq.answer: Create snapshots before deletion. Without snapshots, deleted resources cannot be recovered. Enable AWS Backup for critical data.

faq.question: What is the recommended cleanup schedule?
faq.answer: Weekly discovery with monthly cleanup is recommended. This gives teams time to identify and claim resources they still need.

faq.question: Does this work with AWS Backup?
faq.answer: The skill complements AWS Backup by cleaning up resources that are not protected by backup policies. Use both together for complete coverage.
